Reviews Contact
Home / Services / Emergency Care

Emergency Care

Urgent veterinary services for Asheville pet emergencies.

When accidents happen on Asheville's trails or sudden illness strikes, our emergency team provides prompt, skilled care. We're equipped to handle common local emergencies like hiking injuries on the Mountains-to-Sea Trail, toxin exposures from native plants, or winter-related conditions.

Our facility maintains extended hours to serve Asheville pet owners when they need us most. We stock antivenom for snake bites common in our region and have experience treating altitude-related issues in flatland pets new to the area.

For after-hours emergencies, our on-call system ensures an Asheville-based veterinarian is always available. We coordinate closely with regional specialists when advanced care is needed, streamlining referrals for Buncombe County pet owners.

Our Process

  1. 01 Step 1: Immediate triage assessment
  2. 02 Step 2: Stabilization procedures as needed
  3. 03 Step 3: Diagnostic testing using in-house equipment
  4. 04 Step 4: Treatment plan and follow-up coordination

Benefits

  • Quick response to critical situations
  • Local knowledge of regional hazards
  • Extended hours for urgent needs
  • Seamless specialist referrals

Frequently Asked Questions

What are common Asheville pet emergencies?

We frequently see porcupine quill injuries, hiking trauma, mushroom toxicoses from our forests, and winter exposure cases in outdoor pets.

Should I call before bringing in an emergency?

If possible, yes - this helps us prepare. But in truly critical situations, come immediately and we'll assess upon arrival.

Book an Appointment

Ready to get started with emergency care? Contact us today.

Schedule Now Or call: 555-vet-r92
